# UC-22.18.11 · SCADA command authentication — control point 4

## Description

Evidences API RP 1164 cybersecurity practices for pipeline SCADA using OT and engineering audit sources.

## Value

Evidences API RP 1164 cybersecurity practices for pipeline SCADA by monitoring "SCADA command authentication — control point 4" using OT and engineering audit sources.

## Implementation

(1) Confirm field extractions and CIM tags for the sourcetypes in scope; (2) Load or maintain the referenced lookups/KVStore collections with owner attestation dates; (3) Schedule the search at an interval aligned to the control’s materiality; (4) Route positive findings to the compliance ticketing queue with required evidence fields; (5) Retain scheduled search exports per records management and legal hold procedures. Indexes required: `index=scada`.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk OT Security Add-on (5151), Splunk Industrial Asset Intelligence, Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Edge Hub, Nozomi Networks TA for Splunk (6905), Splunk Add-on for CyberArk (4295), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621)..
• Ensure the following data sources are available: RTU/PLC, DNP3, Modbus OPC-UA, Wonderware/Ignition, historian, OT firewall, wireless sensor feeds.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Confirm field extractions and CIM tags for the sourcetypes in scope; (2) Load or maintain the referenced lookups/KVStore collections with owner attestation dates; (3) Schedule the search at an interval aligned to the control’s materiality; (4) Route positive findings to the compliance ticketing queue with required evidence fields; (5) Retain scheduled search exports per records management and legal hold procedures.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=scada sourcetype=ignition:audit earliest=-30d
| eval uc_tag="22.18.11"
| where match(action,"(?i)pump") AND match(source,"(?i)script")
| stats count by action, user, project_name
| sort - count
```

Understanding this SPL

**Ignition pump actions originating from scripts (API RP 1164)** — Evidences API RP 1164 cybersecurity practices for pipeline SCADA using OT and engineering audit sources.

Documented **Data sources**: RTU/PLC, DNP3, Modbus OPC-UA, Wonderware/Ignition, historian, OT firewall, wireless sensor feeds. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk OT Security Add-on (5151), Splunk Industrial Asset Intelligence, Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Edge Hub, Nozomi Networks TA for Splunk (6905), Splunk Add-on for CyberArk (4295), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: scada; **sourcetype**: ignition:audit. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=scada, sourcetype=ignition:audit, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **uc_tag** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Filters the current rows with `where match(action,"(?i)pump") AND match(source,"(?i)script")` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by action, user, project_name** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the OT historian (PI, Proficy) or the vendor HMI/operations console the operator trusts. Spot-check a few source events, reconcile counts, and verify field extractions, thresholds, and index permissions for the people who need this evidence.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Time chart, Table, Single value, Node-link or sankey (optional)

## SPL

```spl
index=scada sourcetype=ignition:audit earliest=-30d
| eval uc_tag="22.18.11"
| where match(action,"(?i)pump") AND match(source,"(?i)script")
| stats count by action, user, project_name
| sort - count
```
